Then run a line set from the condenser to the branch box and make all the final connections.

Amenities Renovations
â¢	Selective interior demolition of existing finishes, flooring, millwork, ceilings, lighting fixtures, mirrors, signage, and non load bearing elements for replacement in kind or cosmetic upgrade.
â¢	Replacement and refinishing of interior finishes, including flooring, wall finishes, ceiling finishes, and decorative elements.
â¢	Removal and reinstallation of architectural millwork, casework, lockers, counters, mirrors, acoustic panels, and wall features in updated locations without reconfiguring room layouts.
â¢	Lighting and electrical work limited to fixture replacement and relocation, utilizing existing branch circuits and junction boxes where possible.
â¢	Minor MEP work limited to fixture replacement or reconnection, including plumbing fixtures, diffusers, sprinklers, and fire alarm devices, all within the existing systems and infrastructure.
â¢	Courtyard refresh work, including cleaning and resealing pavers, refinishing existing benches, replacing lighting in kind, and installing updated amenities without modifying site grading or structure.
â¢	Accessibility improvements within the scope of work, ensuring new finishes, fixtures, and equipment comply with applicable ADA and 521 CMR requirements.

Full Scope of Work â Description  Interior renovation and structural improvements to an existing two-family residential building. Work includes basement excavation and underpinning to increase ceiling height, installation of new drainage systems, relocation of utilities, and construction of new structural elements as required by approved plans.  First-floor renovations include reconfiguration of interior spaces, structural beam installation, new kitchen and bathroom work, updated electrical, plumbing, HVAC systems, and finish improvements.  Second-floor work consists of partial interior renovations including kitchen and bathroom updates, structural modifications as required, mechanical and electrical upgrades, and interior finish work.  Exterior work includes removal of the existing unsafe rear stair and construction of a new code-compliant exterior spiral staircase to provide safe egress in accordance with approved plans.  All work to be performed in accordance with approved architectural and structural drawings, Massachusetts State Building Code, and local inspection requirements.
